name: side-eye
description: Agent-first macOS screenshot CLI. Captures displays, windows, or regions with baked-in overlays (grids, bounding boxes) and returns structured JSON. All coordinates use a Local Coordinate System where (0,0) is the top-left of the captured target.

tools.name: side-eye-list
tools.description: Returns the current display topology as JSON, including display count, resolution, scale factor, arrangement, and the name of the active (frontmost) application.

scale_factor.description: Retina scale factor (2.0 for Retina, 1.0 for standard)

arrangement.description: Physical position relative to the main display

tools.name: side-eye-capture
tools.description: Takes a screenshot of a specific display, window, or region. Returns file paths or base64-encoded image data. IMPORTANT: All coordinates (--crop, --draw-rect, --grid) use the Local Coordinate System (LCS) where (0,0) is the top-left of the captured target, NOT the global macOS screen position. When using --crop or --draw-rect, specify pixel coordinates relative to the captured image, not global screen coordinates.

target.description: What to capture. Use 'main' for the primary display, 'external' for the first external monitor, 'external 1'/'external 2' for specific externals by position, 'user_active' for the display with the focused app, 'selfie' for the display hosting this process, 'mouse' for the display under the cursor, 'all' for every display, or a saved zone name.

window.description: If true, capture only the targeted window instead of the full display. Window is captured with transparent background.

base64.description: If true, skip file I/O and return the image as a base64-encoded string in the JSON response. Ideal for sending screenshots directly to an LLM.

crop.description: Crop the captured image. Named styles: 'top-half', 'bottom-half', 'left-half', 'right-half', 'top-left', 'top-right', 'bottom-left', 'bottom-right', 'center'. Or exact pixel coordinates as 'x,y,w,h' in the Local Coordinate System (relative to captured target, NOT global screen).

format.description: Image format. PNG is lossless (larger), JPG/HEIC are lossy (smaller).

quality.description: Compression quality for lossy formats. high=1.0, med=0.6, low=0.3.

show_cursor.description: Include the system cursor in the captured image.

highlight_cursor.description: Draw a translucent 50px circle at the cursor position. Value is a hex color (#RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA). Omit for no highlight.

radius.description: Only with target='mouse'. Captures a box of <radius>*2 pixels centered on the cursor. The cursor will be at the center of the resulting image.

interactive.description: Show the native macOS crosshair selection UI. The human drags a rectangle and that region is captured. Returns bounds in the JSON response.

wait_for_click.description: Block until the human left-clicks, then capture. Returns click_x and click_y in the JSON response (in LCS coordinates). Requires Accessibility permission.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for --interactive and --wait-for-click. Returns a TIMEOUT error if exceeded.

delay.description: Wait N seconds before capturing. Useful for menus, tooltips, or animations.

clipboard.description: Also copy the final image to the system clipboard.

grid.description: Overlay a coordinate grid onto the image. Format: 'COLSxROWS' (e.g., '10x10'). Grid lines are labeled with pixel coordinates in the LCS. Extremely useful for helping LLMs identify element positions.

draw_rect.description: Draw stroke bounding boxes on the image. Each item is an object with 'coords' (x,y,w,h string in LCS) and 'color' (#RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA). Coordinates are in the Local Coordinate System, relative to the final image (post-crop).

draw_rect_fill.description: Draw filled/translucent bounding boxes. Same format as draw_rect but boxes are filled instead of stroked.

thickness.description: Stroke width in pixels for grid lines and rect borders. Increase for Retina displays.

shadow.description: Drop shadow on all drawn elements. Format: 'offsetX,offsetY,blur,#color'. Example: '2,2,4,#00000080'. Helps overlays pop against busy backgrounds.

cursor.description: Cursor position in LCS pixel coordinates (present when --highlight-cursor is used and cursor is in the captured area)

bounds.description: Selection bounds in LCS pixels (present when --interactive is used)

click_x.description: Click X in LCS pixels (present when --wait-for-click)

click_y.description: Click Y in LCS pixels (present when --wait-for-click)

warning.description: Non-fatal issue (e.g., window was minimized, fell back to display capture)

tools.name: side-eye-zone-save
tools.description: Save a named spatial zone (a crop region on a specific display) for repeated capture.

name.description: Zone name (used as capture target later)

bounds.description: Crop region as x,y,w,h in pixel coordinates

tools.name: side-eye-zone-list
tools.description: List all saved zones as JSON.

tools.name: side-eye-zone-delete
tools.description: Delete a saved zone by name.

error_codes.NO_DISPLAY: Cannot find or resolve the target display
error_codes.NO_WINDOW: No suitable window found on the target display
error_codes.SELFIE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find the window of the process hosting side-eye
error_codes.UNKNOWN_TARGET: Target name not recognized and not a saved zone
error_codes.INVALID_COLOR: Hex color string is malformed (use #RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA)
error_codes.INVALID_CROP: Crop style not recognized (use named style or x,y,w,h)
error_codes.INVALID_FORMAT: Image format not recognized (use png, jpg, or heic)
error_codes.INVALID_QUALITY: Quality level not recognized (use high, med, or low)
error_codes.SELECTION_CANCELLED: Interactive selection was cancelled or no region selected
error_codes.INTERACTIVE_UNAVAILABLE: Interactive overlay cannot acquire focus in this context
